name: Continue The Active PTX One-K 128x128 Control-Path Exploit

summary.name: Continue The Active PTX One-K 128x128 Control-Path Exploit
summary.hypothesis: Round 15 kept the restored PTX microkernel on top and produced a small but real scheduler-facing nudge in the right direction: runtime improved from 24.17859173 ms to 24.17100811 ms, barrier eased from 5.48% to 5.21%, and long-scoreboard eased from 7.16% to 7.08%, while active warps stayed pinned around 16.6%. That is too small to replace the accepted base and still leaves the branch about 0.0067 ms behind the 24.16427231 ms best-known run, so the family should be treated as a flat-positive continuation rather than a closed win. The best next move is therefore one more bounded PTX control-path exploit on the current winner surface, explicitly avoiding the already measured B-first prologue, issue-grouping replay, A-then-B handoff refill, and pure future_tile_k hoist variants.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Residual cp.async handoff and inner-loop control overhead in the active 128x128 PTX hot-band microkernel, not DRAM bandwidth saturation and not another restore-only action.
summary.risk: Moderate. The family is still live and slightly positive, but another tweak must stay narrowly distinct from the already closed control-order, handoff-order, and address-hoist sub-variants.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime versus the 24.178592 ms accepted base and the 24.164272 ms best-known run
ranking_notes: The latest measured run is effectively a flat-positive result on the PTX winner surface rather than a clean family closeout.
This remains the highest-priority live frontier family once absorbed restore/export families and the closed handoff replay are filtered out.
It ranks first because it stays on the measured best surface while still targeting the only counters that moved in the right direction last round.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if the next implementation replays a previously measured prologue, handoff, issue-grouping, or pure address-hoist sub-variant, or if another bounded control tweak stays runtime-flat without improving the scheduler signature.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
